evals.prompt: Create a commissioning plan for a Tier IV hyperscale data center with 2N power redundancy. The facility has 4 utility feeds from 2 independent substations, 8 x 3MW diesel generators (4 per power train), 8 UPS modules (4 per path), and 4 cooling plants. The IST must include actual utility disconnection to verify full transfer sequence. Independent third-party CxA is engaged. Target: IT load deployment in 6 months.
evals.expected_output: A Level 1-5 commissioning plan with factory witness testing for UPS, generators, and MV switchgear. Level 2 installation verification with NETA acceptance testing. Level 3 functional tests for each system independently at 25/50/75/100% load. Level 4 IST with actual utility disconnection scenarios including complete utility loss, generator failure during outage, redundant path transfer, and cascade failure. Tier IV fault tolerance verified through simultaneous A and B path testing. Level 5 seasonal commissioning schedule. Timeline fitted to 6-month deployment target.
assertions: Level 1-5 structure is complete with checklists per level
IST includes actual utility disconnection (not ATS test button simulation)
Tier IV fault tolerance is verified through 2N path independence testing
IST scenarios include cascade failure and concurrent failure modes
48-72 hour utility coordination for IST disconnection is noted
Timeline maps L1-L5 progression to 6-month IT deployment target

evals.prompt: Develop a commissioning plan for a modular data center with factory-integrated power and cooling. 10 modular units each 1MW IT capacity. Factory commissioning covers Level 1 (FWT) and partial Level 3 (system-level functional tests). On-site commissioning covers Level 2 (installation and interconnection), remaining Level 3 (inter-module integration), Level 4 (IST), and Level 5 (seasonal). Tier III, N+1 redundancy. CxA is owner's team with DC experience.
evals.expected_output: A commissioning plan adapted for modular delivery with factory Level 1 scope expanded beyond traditional FWT to include system integration testing. On-site Level 2 focused on inter-module connections, utility tie-in, and site infrastructure. Level 3 verifies integration between factory-tested modules. Level 4 IST with N+1 failure scenarios (module failure, utility loss, cooling failure). Factory commissioning protocol reviewed by on-site CxA before factory dispatch.
assertions: Factory Level 1 scope is expanded to include system integration testing
On-site Level 2 focuses on inter-module connections and utility tie-in
Level 3 distinguishes factory-tested functions from site integration functions
Level 4 IST scenarios address modular-specific failures (module isolation, bus connection)
Factory commissioning protocol review by CxA before dispatch is required
N+1 redundancy verification demonstrates module failure without IT load impact

evals.prompt: Create a commissioning plan for a brownfield data center conversion of a former manufacturing facility. Existing building has 480V electrical service (needs upgrade to 13.8kV), existing fire suppression (wet pipe, needs conversion to pre-action/clean agent), and partial BMS (needs upgrade). New equipment: 4 x 2MW UPS, 4 x 2MW generators, 3 cooling plants. Tier III. Unknown condition of existing grounding grid. CxA is independent third party.
evals.expected_output: A commissioning plan with Level 0 assessment of existing systems (electrical, fire, BMS, grounding) before Levels 2-5. Level 0 includes existing grounding grid testing, electrical system survey, fire suppression assessment. Level 1 FWT for all new equipment. Level 2 includes both new equipment installation verification and existing system remediation verification. Level 3 functional tests for new and upgraded systems. Level 4 IST with scenarios specific to brownfield risks (existing system interaction, legacy wiring). Level 5 with enhanced monitoring for first year.
assertions: Level 0 existing systems assessment is included before commissioning scope is finalized
Existing grounding grid testing is specified as a Level 0 priority item
Level 2 covers both new equipment installation and existing system remediation
Brownfield-specific risks (existing wiring, legacy systems interaction) are addressed in IST
Fire suppression conversion from wet pipe to pre-action/clean agent is commissioning scope
Enhanced Year 1 monitoring is specified for Level 5 given brownfield unknowns
